title: VPN for Apple TV: Router Setup, Smart DNS, and the App Store Problem
description: Apple TV has no VPN apps in the App Store. Here is how to use a VPN on Apple TV in 2026 via router setup, Smart DNS, or a virtual router on your Mac.
content:
## The Apple TV VPN Problem

Apple TV (tvOS) does not allow VPN apps in the App Store. Apple's policy blocks VPN providers from publishing a standalone tvOS client, which means there is no NordVPN or Surfshark app you can install directly on the device. This catches a lot of people off guard.

That said, there are three working methods to route your Apple TV through a VPN: configuring your router, using Smart DNS, or sharing a VPN connection from a Mac.

## Method 1: Configure Your Router

If you install a VPN directly on your router, every device connected to that router goes through the VPN automatically. Your Apple TV never needs to know a VPN exists.

This works best if your router supports **OpenVPN** or **WireGuard** natively. Common router firmware that supports this:

- **Asus Merlin** (any Asus router running the Merlin custom firmware)
- **DD-WRT** (many Netgear, TP-Link, and Linksys models)
- **GL.iNet** routers ship with OpenVPN and WireGuard built in, and are popular as travel routers for exactly this purpose

NordVPN, Surfshark, and ExpressVPN all publish router configuration files and step-by-step guides in their support sections. The setup takes about 20 minutes. Once done, your Apple TV unblocks geo-restricted content without any further configuration.

**Downside:** Your entire home network routes through the VPN, including devices where you do not want that. Most VPN providers let you set up split tunnelling on supported routers to exclude specific devices, but this is more complex to configure.

## Method 2: Smart DNS (Easiest Option)

Smart DNS does not encrypt your traffic, but it is the fastest and simplest method for Apple TV specifically. It reroutes DNS queries so that streaming services think you are in a different country.

NordVPN and Surfshark both include Smart DNS with every paid subscription. Setup on Apple TV (4th gen and later running tvOS):

1. Log into your VPN account on a computer or phone.
2. Find the Smart DNS section in account settings and note the two DNS server addresses.
3. On your Apple TV: Settings > Network > Wi-Fi > (your network) > Configure DNS > Manual.
4. Enter the first DNS address from your VPN provider. Save and reboot.

NordVPN's Smart DNS activates after you register your current IP address in the NordVPN account dashboard. If your home IP changes, you need to re-register it.

Smart DNS works reliably for Netflix US, BBC iPlayer, Disney+, and Hulu on Apple TV. It does not hide your IP or stop ISP throttling.

## Method 3: Share a VPN Connection from a Mac

If you have a Mac and do not want to configure your router, you can turn the Mac into a virtual router:

1. Connect the Mac to VPN using the NordVPN or Surfshark app.
2. Go to System Settings > General > Sharing > Internet Sharing.
3. Share the VPN network connection over Ethernet to your Apple TV (requires an Ethernet cable between Mac and Apple TV, or a small switch).

This method works but is inconvenient since the Mac needs to stay on and connected to the VPN whenever you want Apple TV to use it.

## What About Apple TV 4K Gen 4 (2024-2026)?

Apple released the Apple TV 4K (4th generation) in late 2024. It still runs tvOS and still has no VPN app support via the App Store. The three methods above all work on it. Smart DNS remains the quickest to set up.

ExpressVPN discontinued support for its dedicated app on third-party routers in March 2026, so if you have a router configured with an older ExpressVPN setup, switch to manual OpenVPN or WireGuard configuration instead.

## Troubleshooting: VPN Not Working on Apple TV

If you set up Smart DNS and the service still shows your local library:

- Confirm your home IP is registered in the VPN dashboard (it changes when your ISP reassigns it).
- Clear the Apple TV app's cache: delete and reinstall the streaming app.
- Check for DNS caching: reboot the Apple TV after changing DNS settings.
- Some services (Netflix in particular) have begun checking the IP of the DNS resolver itself, not just the browsing IP. If Smart DNS stops working for Netflix, switch to the router VPN method.

## Recommendation

**NordVPN** is the best option for Apple TV because its Smart DNS is easy to configure, it updates blocked IPs quickly, and the support documentation is detailed. If you already have a compatible router, Surfshark's router configuration guides are equally good and the subscription costs less.

For most people, Smart DNS is the right starting point. If you need full traffic encryption for privacy reasons, go with the router method.
